title: Day 22 Quiz — Pre-Interview Revision Check
description: Quick assessment across JavaScript, React, TypeScript, and architecture before the 90-minute machine coding round. Passing is revision — failing means re-read that topic before building.

questions.prompt: What does debounce solve in a search input?

options.text: Delays execution until user stops typing, reducing API calls

questions.explanation: Debounce waits for a pause in input before firing — core search optimization from Week 2.

questions.prompt: Closures allow an inner function to access variables from an outer function even after the outer function has returned.
questions.explanation: Closures are fundamental to hooks, debounce implementations, and many interview questions.
Objects and promises are compared by reference, not value — critical for React state and deps.

questions.prompt: Which cause infinite re-renders? Select all that apply.

options.text: useEffect(() => setCount(c => c + 1)) with no dependency array
useEffect(() => fetchData(), [query]) when query is stable
setState in render body without condition

questions.explanation: Missing deps with setState and setState during render both cause render loops.

questions.prompt: When should you use Redux Toolkit over Context?

options.text: Complex shared state with many actions, middleware, and DevTools needs

questions.explanation: RTK fits complex global state; Context fits low-frequency widely read values like theme.

questions.prompt: Type component props with a TypeScript {{blank1}}. Type API shapes with {{blank2}} matching the JSON contract.
questions.explanation: interface and type both work for props and API models — consistency matters in interviews.

questions.prompt: What is the primary benefit of RTK Query over manual fetch in useEffect?

options.text: Built-in caching, invalidation, and loading/error state

questions.explanation: RTK Query handles server state lifecycle — Week 3 core concept.

questions.prompt: In one sentence: why use array index as key is dangerous in a Kanban task list?
questions.sampleAnswer: Index keys break React's identity tracking when tasks are added, removed, or reordered — causing wrong DOM reuse, lost input state, and incorrect deletes.
questions.explanation: Classic machine coding and debugging interview trap.

questions.prompt: Complete the immutable task status update.
questions.explanation: map returns a new array with one updated item — never mutate prev in place.

questions.prompt: First action in a 90-minute machine coding round?

options.text: Install Redux and RTK Query immediately
Clarify requirements, sketch folder structure, define MVP scope
Build pixel-perfect CSS before any logic

questions.explanation: Interviewers score requirement clarification and planning before lines of code.
